options.a: nothing is off anywhere — every chat checked, no one upset with you
options.b: someone needed you today and you came through
options.c: everyone's accounted for — you know where they are, and they answered fast
options.d: someone took the next decision off your plate

K.A1.stem: In your closest circle, the moment that actually settles you is when…

options.a: peace — they have no reason to be annoyed with you now
options.b: a seat — you're the one they'd call first now, and that seat is yours
options.c: contact — the favor is a reason to be in touch again tomorrow
options.d: credit with the person who handles things for you — you do your part, they carry the heavy part
options.e: the room — everyone saw who saved the day

K.A2.stem: When you go out of your way for someone — and you often do — what does it quietly get you?

options.a: someone's tone was off with you — not angry, just off
options.b: the thing you always handle got handled fine by someone else
options.c: slower replies than usual; plans you weren't in
options.d: being told "you decide" on something that matters

K.A3.stem: The small thing that can quietly ruin your day:

options.a: hit back harder, immediately, in front of everyone — they need to relearn where they stand
options.b: go visibly, unmistakably hot — not calibrated, just burning; people step back and the matter drops
options.c: smile, agree — and start arranging the board: who to talk to, what they don't know you know
options.d: nothing, today — the date, the room, the exact words go into the file

K.AS1.stem: Someone at school/work moves against you openly. Your first competent move:

options.a: winning — pressure until they fold; being on top is the point
options.b: spacing — while you're burning, nobody gets near enough to push a second time
options.c: cover — while they watch the fire, you move the pieces
options.d: it isn't heat — it's cold, and it can wait; the reply arrives when it costs them most
options.e: by the end, half of it has turned on yourself — how you handled it joins the list you hold against you

K.AS2.stem: At your most heated, the real job the heat is doing:

options.a: it's settled — you answered it same-day, at volume; closed matters stay closed
options.b: you're still prickly in every direction — people are being careful around you, which suits you
options.c: somehow, by Friday, the story everyone carries puts you on the right side of it
options.d: you could recite the incident to the minute; nothing has happened yet; something will

K.AS3.stem: A week after someone crossed you:

options.a: corrected on the spot, in front of everyone
options.b: you run hot for days, at everything — people learn to step around you
options.c: you let it stand — and by month's end the pipeline runs through you and they're outside it
options.d: nothing shows; it goes in the file, dated
options.e: the heat never quite reaches them — it lands on you for letting it happen, and the week's treats get quietly cancelled; that feels fair

K.AST.stem: Someone presents your work as theirs.

options.a: no one here has a single reason to dislike you yet — keep it that way
options.b: find who needs a hand with boxes — be established as useful by week two
options.c: the call schedule home is fixed before the wifi is
options.d: pick the room nearest the person who'll know how things work
options.e: make the entrance — by week two the new crowd should have a story about you they're already retelling

K.AT.stem: First month in a new city. Job one:

options.a: raise the work until no one could fault it — third pass, fourth pass; "good enough" is other people's phrase
options.b: the system carries you — same desk, same order, boxes ticked in sequence
options.c: track the field — who's ahead, what the evaluator hinted, what changed since yesterday
options.d: draft the disaster — the version where it all collapses, in detail, with plans B and C built off it
options.e: seal the supply lines — arrange the ten days so that no part of it depends on anyone: no borrowed notes, no favors, no waiting on someone's reply

K.C1.stem: Big deadline in ten days. Your natural mode:

options.a: the flaw-check — one visible mistake and the whole thing can be dismissed
options.b: the sequence itself — done out of order, the day feels unsafe in a way you don't argue with; you redo it
options.c: the sweep — before settling anywhere (room, meeting, group chat), locate the moods, the exits, the likely problem
options.d: the rehearsal — run the worst version start to finish first; walking in without having pre-lived it is walking in unprepared
options.e: the map of the players — who actually decides, who talks to whom, where a well-placed word would move the outcome; you don't walk in without knowing whose game it is

K.C2.stem: The step you can't skip — and what skipping it would mean:

options.a: the thing you already submitted, re-read for the error you might have missed
options.b: tomorrow's list, re-ordered again; the morning sequence fixed
options.c: everyone's status — the group chat re-scrolled, stories checked; the map of where things stand, refreshed once more
options.d: a search spiral on the bad thing that could happen — odds, symptoms, what-if threads

K.C3.stem: It's late; you're not asleep. The tab that's open:

options.a: your leg of the plan gets redone until no one could find fault with it
options.b: packing runs in the fixed order it always runs; break the order, restart it
options.c: you're the one tracking everything live — weather, delays, moods, prices
options.d: you've pre-run the missed-connection / lost-wallet / hospital version, and there's a plan for each
options.e: your pack is a closed system — your own cash reserve, chargers, meds, backups; whatever happens, you won't be borrowing
options.f: by day two the itinerary quietly runs through you — swaps traded, preferences banked, and everyone somehow votes the way you'd already decided

options.a: mid-performance — eyes on you, right now, all of them
options.b: the quiet after — your standing confirmed; things are as they should be
options.c: that night — replaying it alone, building the next, bigger scene onto it

K.E1.stem: The whole room is impressed with you. The moment that actually lands:

options.a: it's half-lost — unseen might as well be undone
options.b: it counts fully — your own ledger is the one that matters
options.c: it becomes material — in your head it's already a chapter of the larger story only you know

K.E2.stem: You achieve something real, and no one will ever see it.

options.a: staged or saved for the moment they'll be shown
options.b: receipts — records of being right, being best, being owed better
options.c: worlds — long private threads of a life no one has seen any part of

K.E3.stem: Your camera roll / drafts / private notes, honestly:

options.a: whether they're still looking
options.b: whether the standing held — did the right people register it
options.c: whether it's keepable — the scene is already being shelved into the private collection

K.ET.stem: Right after you've made a room laugh or gasp, the thing you check:

options.a: you're still in the chat, answering in one-word replies; somewhere in the last ten minutes the feeling switched itself off
options.b: chat muted; food ordered, feed open — the evening gets poured over it until it blurs
options.c: you say the one deliberately explosive thing — the jolt is the first thing you've felt all day
options.d: you put the phone down and physically leave — a walk, a shower, anywhere that isn't the chat

K.N1.stem: A group-chat blow-up, aimed partly at you, mid-evening.

options.a: tasks getting done like a machine; if someone asks, "I'm fine" — and it's true, there's nothing on
options.b: ordered-in food, screen on, snacks in reach; the evening disappears into the feed
options.c: restlessness until you find an edge — a bet, a dare, a 2 a.m. ride, a message you shouldn't send
options.d: from the outside: nothing, a girl at a desk — but the evening went somewhere; the long private storyline picked up exactly where it left off, and hours passed there

K.N2.stem: What an ordinary hard evening looks like from the outside.

options.a: nothing "did" it — you left the feeling behind, like closing a tab
options.b: enough input — taste, sound, scroll — and the volume finally went down
options.c: something with stakes — the body lit up and there was no room left for anything else

K.N3.stem: When it finally quiets down inside, what did the trick?

options.a: extra level — flat voice, business as usual, nothing leaking
options.b: cocooned — takeout, blankets, twelve hours of something streaming
options.c: suddenly signed up for something with real stakes that same week
options.d: thinner on the map — quietly out of the chat, the plans, the rooms where the week could find you
options.e: present but lit from somewhere else — the notebooks/drafts get longer that week, and she smiles at things that didn't happen in this room

K.NT.stem: In a genuinely bad week, the version of you your friends can see is…

options.a: comfort — the exact food, the exact show, the warm bath; turn the feeling down
options.b: voltage — somewhere loud, fast, risky, new; turn something else up until it drowns

K.P1.stem: A free, bad-mood night and money in your pocket:

options.a: what you now are to them — the one they'd trust it to; the first call next time
options.b: the hour itself — every glass raised pointing at you; all of it looking

K.P10.stem: You put on the whole event for your group — planned, cooked, organized — and it's clearly a success. What stays with you:

options.a: stays hot and stays outward — it keeps the area clear while it burns; when they retreat, it's done its job
options.b: turns on you — the outburst becomes one more thing you hold against yourself, and the evening goes to paying for it

K.P11.stem: After you snap at someone, the next hour:

options.a: things stay smooth between you — no edge, no falling-out
options.b: the conversation moves off you — your real opinion stays yours

K.P12.stem: When you agree with something you don't actually agree with, it's mostly so that:

options.a: "it'll go how it goes" — you stop steering and let the week drive over you; if someone helps, fine, but you're not reaching for anyone
options.b: "we'll sort it out" — and we means them: before the week starts you've already put it in your person's hands, the way it always ends up there

K.P13.stem: A week you can't cope with is coming. Which sentence is closer to what actually happens?

options.a: closes the case — you put the effort down; nothing ventured, nothing owed
options.b: opens the drill — you run the wreck in advance and pack for it

options.a: shrink the exposure-time — arrive late, leave early, volunteer for the kitchen, the long bathroom breaks
options.b: full attendance, good form, perfect surface — you're visibly there for all of it, and afterwards they know exactly nothing new

K.P15.stem: An event you can't skip — family thing, mandatory dinner. Your way through it:

options.a: an argument with one specific scene — versions of what you should have said, dated, on loop
options.b: a settled conclusion, restated — how it goes, how it always goes; no scene needed

K.P16.stem: The brooding you do, if someone transcribed it:

options.a: nothing reached you unseen — you'd watched it form, so when it arrived you were already braced
options.b: you were there before they asked — you'd seen who was wobbling, and you showed up with exactly what they needed
options.c: everyone stayed findable — nobody drifted out of reach without you knowing the moment it started
options.d: the next move was obvious — you'd read the table, so when the moment came you played it first
options.e: nothing was off between you and anyone — the one cooling tone got smoothed before it could set

K.P17.stem: All that quiet keeping-track you do — the statuses, the moods, the who-said-what. On the day it pays off, what did it buy you?

options.a: nothing — that's the point; flat, quiet, no content, the feeling switched off like a tab closed
options.b: a whole place — the same one as last time, with its own people and plot, and it was mid-scene waiting for you

K.P18.stem: People sometimes find you "elsewhere." When you go elsewhere, what's there?

options.a: a door — as long as the surface is interesting enough, nobody asks what's behind it; you go home un-known, which is the win
options.b: a game — the surface keeps them looking at the wrong hand; there's something you're after, and the mask is how it gets got
options.c: a peace — the surface is whatever this room needed you to be; no edges, no friction, nobody with a reason to turn on you

K.P19.stem: doubles as the §8.1 Concealer-attractor tax; also the (Concealer, Trickster) pair resolver) — You run a good surface — pleasant, competent, nothing given away. What is the surface holding in place?

options.a: you go along and enjoy what you can — it isn't worth the crease it would cause
options.b: you go along — and end up running it: snacks, tickets, the drive; by Sunday they're saying they couldn't have done it without you

K.P2.stem: A friend plans a weekend you privately hate:

options.a: a cleaner thing — the work itself gets another pass; what you can't hand in is a flaw someone could point to
options.b: a readier you — the work stopped changing an hour ago; what's being built now is the version where it goes wrong and you already know what you'll do

K.P20.stem: The night before something is due, you're still at the desk. What is the extra hour actually producing?

options.a: you'd have to ask — someone would have to be leaned on, owed, waited for; the whole point is that you never need a single hand
options.b: it could be faulted — a visible flaw, a thing someone could point to and dismiss you by
options.c: it could surprise you — the unrehearsed version could arrive, the one you haven't lived through yet
options.d: someone you cover for would be caught out — the people who run on your preparation would be the ones exposed

K.P21.stem: Being the prepared one — it's how you're built. What would actually break if the preparation slipped?

options.a: contact scheduling — fixed call times, location shared, a halfway visit; keep the thread short
options.b: survival scheduling — before they go, they set up everything you'll need: the bills, the decisions, whose hands you'll be in meanwhile

K.P3.stem: Your person is going away for a month. The plan you push for:

options.a: they conceded — apologized, backed down, gave it up; the win is the point, and you'd have pressed until you had it
options.b: they left you alone — nothing was won and nothing needed to be; the space around you was clear again, and that was the whole job

K.P4.stem: Think of the last time your anger actually worked. What did "worked" look like?

options.a: a room of strangers who spend it captivated by you — though they don't matter and you'll never see them again
options.b: the invitation that confirms you've reached the level above — though the evening is dull and nobody looks at you twice

options.a: keeping the picture current — live signals, small shifts, what just changed
options.b: finishing the movie — the checking feeds a story of how it will go, played to the end so nothing can surprise you

K.P6.stem: What your checking is actually doing:

options.a: you get out — an excuse, a bathroom break that doesn't end
options.b: you stay through all of it, answering normally, feeling nothing you could name

K.P7.stem: The conversation you've been dreading starts right now, in front of you:

options.a: they enjoyed you and learned nothing about you — you got home with yourself un-shared
options.b: they couldn't take their eyes off you — you can still feel where the attention sat

K.P8.stem: You're good in a room. Afterwards, what made the evening a success:

options.a: the same scene from last week or last year, replayed for the missed line
options.b: next week's scene, played to its worst ending, again — so you'll be ready

K.P9.stem: You catch yourself deep in a loop. It's usually:

options.a: practicing feeling nothing — emptying your head, or putting something on to blur it out
options.b: building the route around it — which hallway, which excuse, how not to be caught alone with it — or quietly confirming you can carry every piece of it yourself: no favors asked, no one owed
options.c: letting it settle on you — it can't be dodged; you lie under it with your eyes closed
options.d: reaching for your phone — you want one specific person to know about it tonight — or running the roster: who might be upset with you, who'll need looking after because of tomorrow
options.e: drafting the counterattack — they're in the wrong, and tomorrow they'll hear it — or laying out the board in the dark: who to talk to first, what they don't know you know
options.f: restoring altitude — recalling what you are and what they are, until it shrinks — or slipping into the better story: the world where you're already what this one hasn't noticed yet
options.g: listing the branches — every way it can go, a move ready for each, so nothing tomorrow can catch you bare — or walking tonight's fixed order until the day sits safely in its slots

K.R1.stem: Lights out; tomorrow holds something you're dreading. In bed, you notice yourself…

options.a: gone glassy — you're looking out the window, feeling nothing in particular
options.b: locating the exit reason — or going quietly self-contained: whatever this turns into, you won't be needing anything from this room
options.c: thinking "here it comes again" and going quiet under it
options.d: moving your chair closer to someone — or getting busy taking care of the room: refills, the stuck conversation, the one who's gone quiet
options.e: scanning for whoever made it heavy — or already, pleasantly, working out how this table gets turned your way
options.f: taking the room's temperature into your own hands — the joke, the topic-turn, the spotlight-grab — or absenting upward: the room shrinks while the richer place in your head takes the call
options.g: diagnosing the cause and lining up the fix before any of it can reach you — or restoring your own order: seat, glass, sequence, something the mood can't touch

K.R2.stem: Mid-gathering, the room's mood suddenly goes heavy. You catch yourself…

options.a: it's already gone flat — you're picking dinner; the day feels oddly far away
options.b: you're re-routing tomorrow — the seats, the channels, the people you now won't cross — or resolving, again, to handle the whole repair yourself: nobody asked, nobody owed, nobody told
options.c: it plays on loop the whole ride; of course it went that way
options.d: you're already calling someone — tonight shouldn't be spent alone — or you're doing the rounds: smoothing whoever the day scuffed, checking who might be off with you now
options.e: the more it replays, the clearer whose fault it actually was; the message is half-composed — or the quieter countermove is: which ear to reach first, which favor to call in
options.f: you're recounting the parts where you were better than everyone in that room — or the day is already being rewritten into the private version, the one where you're finally seen at true size
options.g: you're writing the checklist that makes it impossible for you to be caught like that again — or re-running tonight's fixed order until the day sits straight

K.R3.stem: On the way home after a day where something went publicly wrong for you…

options.a: by evening it's gone quiet in you; you put something on and the evening happens
options.b: you match their distance — pull back first, say less, need less
options.c: so it's happening again; the phone goes face-down and you sit with it
options.d: you send the follow-up, then check whether they're active elsewhere — or you're combing the last week for what you might have done, drafting the patch-things-up opener
options.e: you draft the reply with edges — they should feel the cost of this — or you say nothing and adjust the standings: who else to warm up, what they'll notice they've lost
options.f: you post the best hour of your week, somewhere they'll see it — or you file them out of the story: the plot you actually live in has better casting
options.g: you reconstruct the last three days for the trigger, so it can't repeat

K.R4.stem: A friend has left you on read since yesterday — unusual for them.

options.a: you let things sit — if it's going to get fixed, someone else will have to start it
options.b: you cancel the things you'd been looking forward to — the treat, the outing; they don't feel earned now
options.c: anyone watching sees you mid-scroll, staring past the screen — back in the moment it went wrong, again
options.d: you shrug and say "figures" — and mean it; you'd already half-planned around it failing

K.S1.stem: Something you tried publicly didn't work out. The next 48 hours, from the outside:

options.a: nowhere — you lie there, tired of it, wishing someone would just take it over
options.b: through the list of what you did wrong, sentence by sentence — you'd never talk to a friend that way, but for you it's simply fair
options.c: back through the same scene, editing it — one more replay and maybe it settles
options.d: forward, flat — whatever you do, next time goes the same

K.S2.stem: 2 a.m., not asleep. Where the head goes:

options.a: someone else will have to catch it now — it's out of your hands
options.b: having docked yourself something — once you've paid, the account feels settled
options.c: you don't, really — putting it down feels like losing the last chance to fix it
options.d: expecting nothing — nothing can fall off a shelf you already emptied

K.S3.stem: When you finally put the effort down, the relief comes from…

options.a: first thought: who will handle the next part — someone will have to
options.b: you don't quite take it — set against the last failure, it doesn't clear the balance
options.c: mid-celebration you're back in last month's scene, re-running it
options.d: you don't unpack it — it won't hold

options.a: minimize contact — the seat, shift, and channel where you're least involved
options.b: be present, pleasant, even active — while making sure nothing about the real you enters circulation
options.c: arrange things so you'll never need to ask any of them for anything

K.W1.stem: A new group (class / team) is forming; you'll spend months with them. Your instinctive setup:

options.a: the event itself — the class, the party, the call; best is not being there
options.b: the second question — the one about yourself, past the surface
options.c: the offer of help — owing someone; being the one who needed

K.W2.stem: The moment that makes you tense in advance:

options.a: "hard to get her to come to anything"
options.b: "comes to everything, easy to have around — and none of us knows one private thing about her"
options.c: "never asks for help; you find out she had a crisis after she's fixed it herself"

K.W3.stem: Friends who've known you for years would say:

options.a: you'd have skipped the whole session if you'd known
options.b: you deliver something warm, polished, and empty
options.c: you give the one that shows you run your life solo

K.WT.stem: An orientation round asks everyone to share "one personal thing."

options.A: that they got something out of it — you were useful right up until you weren't
options.B: that they're simply gone — nothing taken, just the seat left empty

O.B1.stem: Someone who mattered hurt you once — most people have one. Which half aged worse?

options.A: the scanner — reading every new authority for what they'll eventually take
options.B: the waiting — catching yourself asking a permission nobody is demanding anymore

O.B10.stem: You've left a controlling situation — a boss, a household, a partner, any flavor. A year on, what actually followed you out?

options.A: there's a definite someone under it — she just has no room where she's the native
options.B: it's quieter than that — take the blending away, and it's not obvious what's left wanting things

O.B11.stem: You can blend almost anywhere — most people can, more than they admit. When the blending comes off at the end of the day:

options.A: the group has a second chat — smaller, older, and you were never in it
options.B: your closest person in the group has a new closest person

O.B12.stem: Two Tuesday discoveries. Which lands harder?

options.A: circles do this — they set, they seal; there's rarely a why with a face on it
options.B: somebody worked the room — something was said, and you can almost name by whom

O.B13.stem: A circle you were part of has cooled on you — it happens. Your standing explanation:

options.A: range control — close enough to be liked, never close enough to be inspected
options.B: translation fatigue — up close they'd need the whole backstory, and they're not from where you're from, in any sense
options.C: just pacing; it closes on its own

O.B2.stem: You keep a certain distance with new people — most people do. Yours is mostly:

options.A: the engineering — who set it up, who already knew, how you were walked into it
options.B: the display — the thing about you it held up to the light

O.B3.stem: A joke at your expense lands in a group and gets a real laugh. Which residue outlives the evening?

options.A: the memo — a durable note about what people, given access, will do
options.B: the film — the sense it left something on you, as if you're the one who now needs cleaning

O.B3b.stem: After being genuinely wronged, two aftertastes. Which lasts longer in you?

options.A: the one left on the platform — the door that closes, the chair that stays empty
options.B: the one who made it out — and can't stand being the one who did

O.B4.stem: Two scenes in fiction reliably get you. Which gets you worse?

options.A: the phone — the ring itself, the news arriving, the minute everything turns
options.B: the after — the world minus them, and you still in it, alone

O.B5.stem: Someone you love is in a fragile stretch — health, distance, anything. The 3 a.m. version of it is:

options.A: monitor — watch it, look it up, wonder what it's the start of
options.B: manage — who'll see it, from what angle, under what cover story

O.B6.stem: Your body does something new and inconvenient — nothing serious. The reflex that actually runs:

options.A: stop providing and the warmth stops — it was never pointed at you, only at the output
options.B: the math is off and someone knows it — more is being taken than given, and it isn't your ledger
options.C: providing is just how groups work

O.B7.stem: Somewhere — work, group, family — you're clearly valued for what you provide. The thought that visits at the sink afterwards:

options.A: the thing is a magnet — warmth arrives at it, never past it, and you've stopped expecting delivery
options.B: the thing is a screen — it stands where you'd otherwise be looked at, and it had better hold
options.C: the thing is just a thing you're good at

O.B8.stem: People like you for a thing — the skill, the humor, the reliability. The quieter read on it:

options.A: "oh, she's fine — she never needs anything"
options.B: "oh, don't hand her that — she couldn't manage it"

O.B9.stem: Two lines said fondly about someone. Which would grate more, said about you today?

options.ABN: a staying that doesn't expire — the door no longer watched
options.ALN: a table where you're not a guest
options.DEF: the all-clear — fine as-is, even seen in full
options.ED: being gotten — eyes that land on you, no translation needed
options.FAI: the settled verdict that you're good at this — immune to the next test
options.MB: one person you could be fully off-guard with
options.POW: the wheel handled — or the wanting that finally knows its own direction
options.VC: a tomorrow guaranteed to hold no ambush

O.C1.stem: At bottom — if one of these could simply be handed to you, which do you take?

options.A: they were right there all evening — and somehow never quite with you
options.B: the moment they glanced at the time and began gathering their things

O.P1.stem: Two small moments with someone who matters. Which one follows you home?

options.A: they'd been looking straight at you — and not seeing you
options.B: you hadn't crossed their mind at all — you'd simply slipped it

O.P1b.stem: Which is worse to learn afterwards?

options.A: who — whose hand is in this, who knew, who let it through
options.B: what did I miss — which sign, which lock, which ordinary Tuesday it started
options.C: neither; you go straight to cleanup

O.P2.stem: Something bad lands on you — any size. The first question your mind actually runs:

options.A: the one who caused it — the face, the choice, what they got away with
options.B: how ordinary the minute before was — and the seat from which you watched it turn
options.C: your own hands, and what they should have done

O.P2b.stem: In the bad scene your imagination occasionally re-runs (everyone's runs one), the frame that loops:

options.A: "you're so capable" — because the next real test can still take it away
options.B: "you're such a good person" — because they're describing someone you've shown them

O.P3.stem: Two compliments, both sincere. Which one leaves the small splinter?

options.A: a thing to keep sealed — the point is that no one ever gets to see it
options.B: a distrust of your own calls — you double-check yourself where you used to be sure
options.C: a flinch at being handed anything that matters — once was enough

O.P3b.stem: A past misstep still visits now and then — everyone owns one. When yours visits, it arrives mostly as:

options.A: the rooms one: you can pass in any group and belong in none — even mid-laughter some translator in you is still working
options.B: the one-person one: even with someone right there who cares, the signal never quite lands — seen daily, read never

O.P4.stem: Two aches. Everyone has met both — but one of them is yours, the one that's been there longest. Which?

options.A: a table — plural, people, a place where you'd stop performing membership because you'd have it
options.B: a reader — one pair of eyes that finally lands on you without a manual

O.P4b.stem: If exactly one could be delivered tonight:

options.A: deciding is the easy part — you'll have picked by breakfast, alone is fine; it's the verdict you brace for, because your picks have a record of coming back marked wrong
options.B: the deciding itself is the wall — alone, the needle just doesn't move; the same choice with someone beside you would be done in an hour
options.C: neither; decisions come and go fine

O.P5.stem: A real decision, yours alone, due tomorrow. Which is closer to how it actually goes?

options.A: it would all get run — lists made, bills filed, calls placed — and you'd still be bracing for the audit that finds the mistake only you would make
options.B: it wouldn't get run — not run badly, just not run; the machinery turns over when someone is in it with you, and this season no one is

O.P5b.stem: A season completely alone — no one to ask, lean on, or check with. Which is the truer forecast?

options.A: a long thread of friends who've known each other forever — in-jokes stacked like furniture
options.B: "she knew what I meant before I finished saying it" — someone describing being gotten
options.C: a quiet drifting-apart story — or the post that just says "one year without you"
options.D: a straight-A report of a test — the exam aced, the audition passed, the license nailed first try; the verdict-machine smiled on someone, and it would have found you out
options.E: "just show people the real you" — cheerful advice, huge engagement
options.F: a story of someone's kindness repaid with a knife in the back — comments cheering the revenge
options.G: an ordinary dashcam morning that turns, in one second, into the worst day of someone's life
options.H: "signed the lease, filed the taxes, chose the city" — someone steering a whole life with one pair of hands, no co-pilot, and liking it

O.R1.stem: Late one night you're scrolling, and a stranger's post unexpectedly stings. Of these, the one that would sit with you longest:

options.A: the one that listens for people going or cooling — checking whether they're still really with you
options.B: the one that grades you — tallying what you got wrong and what you're about to
options.C: the one that keeps translating you into the local language — everywhere, with everyone
options.D: the one that keeps watch — for the next move against you, or the next blow out of nowhere
options.E: the one that stalls at every fork until someone else weighs in

O.R2.stem: If one background hum in your head could be switched off tomorrow, you'd pick:

options.A: "you always half-expect people to walk out the door"
options.B: "you've never once felt properly understood, have you"
options.C: "you never stop bracing for the next blow"
options.D: "you keep one eye on everyone's hands"
options.E: "you've never really been one of them — any them, anywhere"
options.F: "you still think if they saw all of you, they'd go"
options.G: "you're always waiting to be found out as not good enough at this"
options.H: "you can't quite run it alone, can you — and you know it"

O.R3.stem: Someone who knows you well says one of these — kindly, and not wrongly. Which one would land closest to the bone?

options.A: the sense they're on their way out — you catch yourself reading the shorter replies like tea leaves
options.B: that even when you do talk, it lands beside you — they're present, and not quite with you
options.C: that their real life is a room you're not in — the group, the plans, the jokes that aren't yours
options.D: the itch to find out what's actually going on — what you're not being told

O.S1.stem: A close friend has been quieter than usual for a few weeks. The version of it that actually bothers you:

options.A: the result — botching it where it counts, the record showing it
options.B: the exposure — that many eyes at that range, whatever the result
options.C: the aloneness of it — you can do things, just not unheld; who else is on this?
options.D: the angle — why you, and who benefits if you stumble

O.S2.stem: You're asked, in front of people who matter, to take on something visible and difficult. The needle that moves first:

options.A: authored — someone's intention is in it somewhere: a set-up, a leak, a turn
options.B: authorless — a call, a lump, a skid; the world doesn't need a reason
options.C: it's about the people you'd lose, not the event itself
options.D: it's about being the one who couldn't handle whatever it is

O.S3.stem: Walking home you get the classic bad feeling — something's off. Nine times out of ten, the shape it takes:

options.A: grinding at it — braced the whole way for the mistake you're surely making
options.B: not started — it just sits there; it won't begin until someone is in it with you
options.C: done early, checked five times, and you can still hear the missed error ticking

O.S4.stem: A new, fiddly, consequential form must be filed by Friday. No one can do it for you. Tuesday-you is most likely:

options.A: noticing that one person — the friend across the table who loves you — is answering someone you aren't quite; even your closest pair runs on translation
options.B: noticing you're doing the accent again — fluent company, native of nowhere; or that the things you actually love live in a drawer no group has ever asked about
options.C: noticing one person keeps reading you — closer than you dressed for
options.D: noticing the friend you came with drifting into someone else's orbit tonight

O.S5.stem: A gathering is going fine. The small moment that would quietly deflate you:

options.AG-1: Every slight is logged with date and interest — nothing forgiven, nothing written off, all of it due someday.
options.AG-2: You keep smiling at them for years — holding the friendship, holding the position — waiting for the one moment that repays everything at once.
options.AG-3: What was done to you must be done back — the same wound, in the same place, and they must know why.
options.AV-1: You've engineered a life that needs no encounters — the room, the delivery at the door, the bell you don't answer.
options.AV-2: You map your days around certain streets, people, and topics — life as a standing network of detours.
options.AV-3: The letter stays unopened and the talk never happens — what you never begin can never go wrong.
options.AV-4: You show up, then vanish at the first sign of weather — out of the party, out of the chat, before anything comes to a head.
options.AV-5: You keep the world down to a few safe rooms and routes — new places, new people, new anything: no.
options.AV-6: Since the one time it went wrong, that entire door stays closed — and you live freely everywhere else.
options.CA-1: You run the disaster nightly in full detail — the firing, the exposure, the crash — so the real thing would find you already used to it.
options.CA-2: Every room you enter is a room you've already escaped — doors marked, grab-list ready, someone to call.
options.CA-3: The cough is the illness, the delay is the accident, the short reply is the ending — the darkest reading is the only safe one.
options.CA-4: You walk every option to its ruin before choosing — often the only unwrecked path left is standing still.
options.CL-1: You know their routine, their evening path, what sits by their door — you'd read the signs of leaving before they knew them.
options.CL-2: You poll the bond — are we okay, do you still, did I do something — and every silence gets asked until it answers.
options.CL-3: You keep yourself physically near — going where they go, waiting outside — because distance itself is the threat.
options.CL-4: You bind with tokens and vows — the matching things, the promises extracted, the anniversaries kept like law.
options.CO-1: You've studied ordinary and you wear it — clothes, opinions, laughter all tuned to the middle of the flock.
options.CO-2: One pleasant face answers everything — the smile and the "I'm fine" that close every door before it opens.
options.CO-3: Each circle of your life gets a different slice of you — and no two circles ever meet.
options.CO-4: You answer every question and reveal nothing — replies that are true and empty, questions returned as questions.
options.CO-5: There's exactly one thing that stays buried, whatever it costs — everything else about you can be shown freely.
options.CT-1: You arrive already carrying what they were about to want — the food, the fix, the fare.
options.CT-2: You're strongest at their weakest — drawn to the broken, indispensable in their worst hour, oddly lost in their best.
options.CT-3: You hold the keys, the schedule, the passwords — cutting you out would collapse the whole thing, and that's how you've built it.
options.DM-1: You've already answered every question with no — won't apply, won't confess, won't ask — so nothing ever has to be risked.
options.DM-2: You grieve things while you still have them — the goodbye said early and quietly, so nothing can be torn from you later.
options.DM-3: "People like me don't get that" isn't a complaint — it's solid ground, and you stand on it.
options.DM-4: Everything's rotten, everyone settles, it all ends the same — and if nothing is worth anything, you've lost nothing.
options.DP-1: You've handed one vetted person everything — the choosing, the carrying, the keys to your life — and bound the arrangement tight.
options.DP-2: You ride with whoever's driving — plans, opinions, orders supplied by the current strong one; you're no trouble at all.
options.DP-3: You lead with the tremble — the visible "I can't" that summons hands before you touch the ground.
options.DT-1: The moment it hits, you go level — steady voice, steady hands, business as usual while everything burns.
options.DT-2: Your first move is to shrink it — nothing really happened, it's fine, everyone's making too much of this.
options.DT-3: You turn the feeling into a problem — dissect it, sort it, explain it — until there's nothing left over to feel.
options.DT-4: You stay in the room but step out of yourself — watching the scene happen to someone wearing your face.
options.FA-1: Real life is the prologue — the day is coming when they all find out what you actually are.
options.FA-2: Your real people, your real world, are elsewhere — this one is a waiting room you're just passing through.
options.FA-3: You keep a second world built and furnished in daily detail — and it outranks the one outside.
options.FA-4: You narrate your life as a hidden epic — every humiliation a chapter, every setback a trial on the arc toward your rise.
options.PE-1: The center of the room is just where you naturally stand — the show doesn't stop, because you're always on.
options.PE-2: You're the funny one — if the table is laughing because of you, the evening counts as won.
options.PE-3: You go for the gasp — the marvel, the "how did you even DO that" — because awe can't be faked or withheld.
options.PE-4: You'd rather be stared at than unseen — shock works, scandal works, any gaze is warmer than none.
options.PE-5: You maintain the version of you they get to see — the feed, the angle, the persona, curated like an exhibit.
options.PF-1: Nothing leaves your hands with a flaw in it — triple-checked, immaculate, redone until it can't be faulted.
options.PF-2: Whatever comes, you've already practiced it — rehearsed past any need, packed for contingencies nobody imagined.
options.PF-3: Never late, never rude, never owing — your record is a life with no handle to grab.
options.PL-1: You arrive as whoever the room needs — tastes, opinions, even your laugh adjusted to the present company.
options.PL-2: You agree early, apologize first, absorb the blame — any anger in the room has to be put out, whatever it costs you.
options.PL-3: You take every request, every shift, every favor — one no might be the last straw.
options.RI-1: One more check buys another hour of safety — the lock, the stove, the sent message re-read twice.
options.RI-2: The day holds as long as the sequence holds — the fixed morning order, the goodnight rite, the steps in their proper order.
options.RI-3: You scrub, tidy, arrange — clean enough, ordered enough, and it can't touch you.
options.RS-1: Your plan for the worst is to hold still and stay findable — someone is coming, and your part is to still be there.
options.RS-2: Under threat you go small and still — no plan, no motion, just waiting for it to pass over.
options.RS-3: You go where you're pushed and take what you're given — the current decides, it always does anyway.
options.RU-1: You keep booking the same visiting-room — the person, the friendship, the era, replayed for one more hour inside it.
options.RU-2: You rerun the scene with edits — this time you say the right thing, catch the tell, take the other door.
options.RU-3: You re-argue the case you lost — gathering evidence, perfecting an argument no court will ever hear.
options.RU-4: You keep everything — diaries, screenshots, saved chats — the past filed, curated, and revisited on schedule.
options.SP-1: You say the cruel thing about yourself first, hardest, in front of everyone — no one can read you a verdict you've already read out.
options.SP-2: The nicer the thing, the less you let yourself have it — the rest, the gift, the good dessert, all quietly declined.
options.SP-3: You work the debt — extra shifts, favors, service — repaying something you've priced at unpayable.
options.SP-4: Right at the threshold of a good thing, you break it — the win ruined, the message unsent, late to your own chance.
options.SR-1: You do everything yourself — help offered is help declined, politely, every time.
options.SR-2: You stockpile against ever needing anyone — savings, supplies, skills, the spare of everything.
options.SR-3: The heavier the load, the harder you refuse the hand — you'd rather drop it than pass it.
options.SR-4: You only trust what you've checked yourself — advice gets thanked, discarded, and re-derived from scratch.
options.SS-1: You handle the worst nights by the mouthful — the fridge at midnight, the third helping, no room left inside for anything else.
options.SS-2: You reach for whatever dims things — a drink, a pill, anything that turns the world down until sleep takes over.
options.SS-3: You keep the feed running — noise in both ears, screen until you drop, never one silent minute for it to speak into.
options.SS-4: You pay each hurt off on the spot — the little purchase, the dessert, the small treat the instant it stings.
options.ST-1: You pick the target, study the weakness, and move when they're alone — cold, chosen, never hot temper.
options.ST-2: At the first sign of threat you hit first and hardest — the fight ends before it exists.
options.ST-3: You keep the fear standing — the tone, the looming, the example made of one so the rest never test you.
options.SV-1: The rules are for other people — you don't demand deference, you just notice when it's late.
options.SV-2: People's time, work, and hands are simply available to you — you use them openly, and owe nothing for it.
options.SV-3: You keep the standings current — best in the room or it doesn't count, and second place is a kind of death.
options.TR-1: You plant the idea, arrange who does what, and win without ever being seen to move.
options.TR-2: You wear whatever face opens the door — charm as toolkit, sincerity as costume.
options.TR-3: The trail was arranged well in advance — evidence placed, fall-guy chosen — so it was never going to lead to you.
options.TS-1: You go find the edge — height, speed, the dare taken first — because the body's alarm is the one thing that still registers.
options.TS-2: You put something real on the line — the all-in bet, the deadline ridden to the last hour — because nothing counts until it can be lost.
options.TS-3: You book the scary thing yourself and arrive early — a danger with an appointment can't ambush you.
options.WA-1: Any approach to the soft place gets heat — you're spiky on contact, and that's the fence doing its job.
options.WA-2: The instant fault lands on you it's already leaving — "it was YOU," "they made me" — out of your mouth before any verdict can settle.
options.WA-3: You're always mid-grievance — at war with the service, the system, the neighbors — and nothing gets near a soft place in weather like this.
options.WT-1: Seat facing the door, exits counted, everyone's position tracked — the unwatched direction is the one it comes from.
options.WT-2: You read faces, tones, the weight of a pause — you see the turn coming in someone before they feel it themselves.
options.WT-3: Nothing moves in your world unknown to you — who said what to whom, what changed since yesterday.

V.CPICK.stem: Which of these is most you?

options.ABN-G1: The leaving actually happened — someone real was there and then wasn't, and you were there for it.
options.ABN-G2: No one exactly left — you just kept finding, without any scene, that your place hadn't been held.
options.ABN-G3: They mostly stayed — that's the thing — and some part of you stayed braced the whole time for the going.
options.ALN-G1: It's been there from the start — wherever you were put, the people around you ran on settings you didn't come with.
options.ALN-G2: It was never anything about you — where you come from, when you arrived, what you left behind did the placing, every time.
options.ALN-G3: You did get in — that's the strange part — and being in is exactly how you learned it wasn't yours.
options.DEF-G1: Yours has no date and no witness — it's something on the inside of you, and nobody has ever actually seen it.
options.DEF-G2: Yours is a fact — something that happened, or the house you come from — kept covered because uncovered it would change every face around you.
options.DEF-G3: Yours sits where seeing happens — in the mirror, in their reactions, in something once said to your face — and you've always known exactly what has to be managed.
options.ED-G1: Nothing much ever came back at all — nobody was cruel, the attention around you was simply never on you.
options.ED-G2: Something always came back — things, praise, affection even — just never quite the thing you were actually asking for.
options.ED-G3: It got settled early that you weren't one of the ones who need things — and whatever you did need stayed unclaimed.
options.FAI-G1: There's an actual day you could name — one time it truly went wrong — and what you believe you're capable of runs through it.
options.FAI-G2: The measure was never yours — it was set beside you, ahead of you, over you, before you had a say — and you keep arriving under it.
options.FAI-G3: No single day did it — it's the standing, everyday version: a gap that has simply always been there, however well you work around it.
options.MB-G1: What came to you sweetly was never just sweet — kindness, in your history, always turned out to be part of a transaction.
options.MB-G2: It was the close ones who did it — whoever hurt you worst got in through trust you'd already given.
options.MB-G3: It wasn't one traitor — it was the ground itself: home, street, school, being unguarded anywhere simply cost.
options.POW-G1: One way or another the steering was never handed over — someone else always covered the deciding, and the practice never got to be yours.
options.POW-G2: You weren't kept from it so much as relieved of it — whenever it mattered, something or someone took over before you could.
options.VC-G1: It actually reached you — your own life took the hit once, or missed it by a breath — and you've known ever since what can happen.
options.VC-G2: You weren't the one it hit — but it lived close enough, long enough, that being braced became your resting state.

V.OGROUP.stem: Which of these is closest to how it was for you? Pick the nearest.

options.ABN-1: You catch yourself reading the signs — shorter replies, earlier goodbyes — long before anyone says they're leaving.
options.ABN-10: The visits got shorter and the gaps got longer — you learned to read a calendar before you could read a clock.
options.ABN-2: You remember the day someone new arrived, and your chair quietly became someone else's.
options.ABN-3: There was a day someone was supposed to come back for you — you can still describe the door you watched.
options.ABN-4: One day they simply weren't there — no goodbye, and nobody had asked you anything first.
options.ABN-5: Houses, schools, almost-parents — at some point you stopped bothering to memorize names.
options.ABN-6: The good days were completely real — which is exactly what made listening for the front door unbearable.
options.ABN-7: You remember waiting somewhere long after everyone else was collected — and deciding not to mention it.
options.ABN-8: You loved someone while the whole house waited to lose them — hello and goodbye were the same season.
options.ABN-9: Someone who loved you used leaving as a threat — and you believed it, every single time.
options.ALN-1: You've watched people click with each other your whole life — and waited for it like a bus that never comes.
options.ALN-10: Some part of you has always been waiting for your real people to come collect you.
options.ALN-11: You can fit in anywhere — and you've noticed that's not the same as belonging somewhere.
options.ALN-2: Every group you've ever joined had already formed by the time you got there.
options.ALN-3: Other kids' houses ran on rules yours didn't — you translated between two worlds and held a passport to neither.
options.ALN-4: You were at every gathering — and can't remember once being asked what you thought.
options.ALN-5: The thing you love most, you learned to stop mentioning by age twelve.
options.ALN-6: Each side has a word for what you are — and it's the other side's name.
options.ALN-7: One week the conversations just started happening without you — and no one ever said why.
options.ALN-8: At ten you could handle things adults praised you for — and not one thing the kids around you were doing.
options.ALN-9: You left the world you were raised in — and the new one never issued you papers.
options.DEF-1: People say you're so kind — and every time, something in you answers: if only you knew.
options.DEF-10: What happened wasn't your doing — but you're the one who has felt dirty ever since.
options.DEF-11: You can still see the exact expression they made — you've been arranging yourself to never cause it again.
options.DEF-12: Someone who knew you best once told you exactly what you are — and you've been quoting them against yourself ever since.
options.DEF-2: You've never told anyone how much you actually want — the amount is the ugly part.
options.DEF-3: There are thoughts you'd rather die than say out loud — and you're sure they mean something about you.
options.DEF-4: There is one specific thing you did — or didn't do — that no one will ever hear from you.
options.DEF-5: Someone didn't make it and you did — and no "it wasn't your fault" has ever once landed.
options.DEF-6: You can't point to why — you've just always known there's something in you that other people don't have to hide.
options.DEF-7: There's one thing you're never seen without — and being seen without it is the actual nightmare.
options.DEF-8: You have a way of standing, dressing, sitting — an entire system — built around one thing about your body.
options.DEF-9: There was a reason you never let anyone walk you home.
options.ED-1: You brought something to show them, and their face never once turned your way.
options.ED-10: Your childhood dinners were mostly eaten alone — nobody did anything wrong, and nobody was there.
options.ED-2: You said how you felt, and what came back was a thing — a gift, a treat, money — never someone listening.
options.ED-3: You did worse and worse things, waiting for someone to finally get angry — and no one ever grabbed your hand.
options.ED-4: You explained what you really meant and watched it land as something else — every time, with people who loved you.
options.ED-5: The one time you said "I can't," it was laughed off — so you never said it again.
options.ED-6: You know everyone's secrets; you'd have to think hard to name one person who knows one of yours.
options.ED-7: Your news was always delivered into a room already worried about something bigger.
options.ED-8: You remember being a child comforting an adult who looked straight through you.
options.ED-9: You can name the exact thing they loved you for — and the warmth stopped the moment you stopped producing it.
options.FAI-1: Every time you succeed, your first thought is how narrowly you got away with it.
options.FAI-10: Somewhere back there you missed the part everyone else learned — and everything since sits on top of the hole.
options.FAI-2: Your report card was never read alone — it was always read next to someone else's.
options.FAI-3: There was one day your whole future was decided — and on that day, you were not good enough.
options.FAI-4: You were completely sure, once, about the thing that mattered most — and you were wrong, and no conclusion of yours has felt solid since.
options.FAI-5: You peaked early — and "so much potential" turned into a bill you can't pay.
options.FAI-6: You watched everyone else simply get it, again and again, while you were still reading the instructions.
options.FAI-7: Someone trusted you with something that mattered, and you dropped it — you've dodged being handed anything since.
options.FAI-8: Your future had a job title before you had a personality — and you're permanently behind schedule on it.
options.FAI-9: You don't fear starting — you fear the moment, always the same one, when you'll quietly stop.
options.MB-1: When someone turns suddenly sweet on you, you brace — waiting to find out what it's the setup for.
options.MB-10: You write nothing down and say nothing out loud at home — you learned your privacy had already been opened.
options.MB-2: You told exactly one person — and by Monday, everyone knew.
options.MB-3: The person whose job was to protect you decided, when it mattered, that you were the price worth paying.
options.MB-4: You learned early to check what people did against what they said — at home, the two never once matched.
options.MB-5: Someone profited off you and called it love — you could itemize exactly what they took.
options.MB-6: You found out the invitation was a joke — and that everyone except you had known all along.
options.MB-7: The person you'd have defended to the end stood up — on the other side.
options.MB-8: You flinch at gifts — you've paid too much for free things.
options.MB-9: You still sit facing the door — you grew up somewhere that being off guard cost you.
options.POW-1: Asked what you want — just you — your mind offers other people's answers, or none at all.
options.POW-2: The first time no one decided for you, you discovered you didn't know how it's done.
options.POW-3: Tasks other people call small can pile up on you until you can't start any of them.
options.POW-4: You can name who saved you each time — the list of times you saved yourself is the empty one.
options.POW-5: "She can't handle it" got decided before you could try — and the certificate never expired.
options.POW-6: You were loved and looked after — and left completely unable to do it yourself; both of those are true.
options.POW-7: Even now, free to choose, you catch yourself waiting for a permission no one is there to give.
options.VC-1: The worst day of your life started as a completely normal one — and normal has never felt safe since.
options.VC-2: Your own body has dropped you without warning before — you've never fully trusted it again.
options.VC-3: You have stood in a place that was supposed to be permanent and watched it stop existing.
options.VC-4: You grew up listening for the phone at night — the bad thing always came back, never on a date you could circle.
options.VC-5: You were one step, one second, one lane away, once — and you've never been able to un-know it.
options.VC-6: It didn't happen to you — you just had to watch, and "it could have been anyone" is the part that never left.
options.VC-7: One season your family had a life, and the next it was gone — you still guard things no lock can hold.
options.VC-8: Nothing ever actually happened — you were simply taught, daily, that something was about to.

V.OPICK.stem: Which of these do you know from the inside? Pick the one that is most yours.
